target.title: fix(status): LINE /status intermittent no-response — fallback, timeout, and retry hardening
target.body:
﻿## Summary  Fixes LINE `/status` intermittently returning no response by hardening the dynamic module loaders, adding a safety-net timeout, and fixing CI failures.  ### Changes (8 commits, +221/-59 across 11 files)  #### Core Status Fixes  | Phase | What | Rationale | |:------|:-----|:---------| | **Phase 0+1** | `.catch(() => undefined)` fallbacks on all 4 dynamic runtime loaders | Previously, a transient import failure would propagate uncaught ??now each loader returns sensible defaults instead of throwing | | **Phase 2** | Eliminate 2 fragile dynamic-import wrappers (`status-message.runtime.ts`, `status-queue.runtime.ts`) via precise static imports; fix `??=` cached-rejection on remaining 3 loaders by resetting promise to `null` on reject | Static imports eliminate the dynamic-hop failure point; promise reset removes the permanent-cache poison so transient failures are retried | | **Phase 2 fix** | Replace `?? {} as any` false-positive in plugin health line with proper early-return guard | Previously a loader failure would show "??Plugins: OK" instead of "?蹎? Plugins: health unavailable" | | **Phase 3** | Upgrade `logVerbose` ??`warn` in `logLineHttpError` for production visibility; add 10s `setTimeout` race in `buildStatusReply` | Without the timeout, a hanging render produces zero response to the user; with `logVerbose` the error was invisible in production logs |  #### CI Fixes  | Fix | What | Rationale | |:----|:-----|:---------| | **Cherry-pick upstream** | Cherry-pick `ccdec2e294` ??`withTaskRegistryTempDir` signature: `() => Promise<T>` ??`(root: string) => Promise<T>` | Fixes 4 pre-existing TS errors in `task-registry.test.ts` from upstream callers passing `(root) =>` | | **OOM mitigation** | Set `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=1` for core-tooling CI shard | Prevents OOM (2?8GB vitest processes on 16GB runner) |  #### Tests  | Suite | Tests | Coverage | |:------|:------|:---------| | `commands-status.test.ts` | 19 ??| Timeout path (new), throw path, and all existing scenarios | | `status-text.test.ts` | 7 ??| `??=` retry pattern (new, fail ??retry ??succeed), channel features, plugin health | | `send.test.ts` | 14 ??| Updated assertions for `warnMock` | | `task-registry.test.ts` | 77 ??| All upstream tests pass after cherry-pick | | TypeScript (`pnpm tsgo`) | 0 errors ??| All 4 build targets pass cleanly |  ### Key Fix Details  **The "no response" root cause** ??`buildStatusText` makes 4 dynamic imports using the `??=` pattern that caches the promise permanently. If any import transiently fails (network hiccup, module compilation delay), the rejection is cached forever and the status command gets stuck on every subsequent attempt. Phase 2 fixes this by resetting the promise cache on rejection.  **The timeout safety net** ??Even with the import fix, a segment of the status rendering could hang indefinitely. The 10s timeout in `buildStatusReply` ensures the user always gets a response with a fallback error message.  **CI hardening** ??Two pre-existing CI failures unrelated to our code were resolved: a TypeScript signature mismatch from upstream commits, and an OOM condition on the core-tooling shard caused by parallel 8GB vitest processes.  ### Closes Closes openclaw/openclaw#94626  ---  ### Types of Changes - [x] ?? Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue) - [ ] ??New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality) - [ ] ???Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change) - [ ] ?? Documentation (non-code change that improves documentation) - [ ] ???Chore (refactoring, test updates, CI configuration changes)  ## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** LINE `/status` command intermittently returned no response when dynamic imports through the `??=` pattern suffered a transient failure — the rejected promise was cached permanently, causing every subsequent `/status` attempt to hang indefinitely. Additionally, the timeout safety net was missing entirely: if any segment of the status rendering stalled, the user received zero response.

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Windows 11 24H2
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.14.1
- **Branch:** `fix/line-94626-status` (commit `6f4f200f32`)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Send `/status` command to the production LINE bot (爬蟲蝦/scraper_claw) running the patched OpenClaw.

```
# 1. Open LINE app
# 2. Find bot 爬蟲蝦 (scraper_claw)
# 3. Send /status command
# 4. Wait for bot reply
```

**Evidence after fix:** Screenshot of live LINE conversation with bot on LINE after the patch:

> [LINE /status proof screenshot](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94806#issuecomment-4750535513)

```
# LINE bot: 爬蟲蝦(scraper_claw)
# /status response:
#   OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (844f405)
#   Gateway: 1d 1h | System: 1d 1h
#   Model: opencode/...
#
# Result: Complete /status response returned
# No hang, no timeout, no empty reply
```

**Observed result after fix:** The LINE bot running OpenClaw with the patched code returns a complete `/status` response without hanging or timing out. Previously the bug caused `/status` to silently produce no response after a transient dynamic import failure.

**What was not tested:**
- Cross-platform runtime testing (Windows 11 only)
- The core-tooling OOM fix can only be verified in CI (runner resource constraints)
target.author: samson1357924
